stakeholders.Role: Leads the implementation of elderly care reforms, regulating all public and subsidized residential care homes.

Source metadata.extraction_context: The Social Welfare Department (SWD) leads the implementation of elderly care reforms, regulating all public and subsidized residential care homes. SWD oversees licensing and compliance for 450+ facilities serving 30,000+ seniors.

stakeholders.Role: Provides acute medical support but faces capacity shortages, often transferring stable elderly patients to NGO-run homes.

Source metadata.extraction_context: Hospital Authority (HA): Manages public hospitals, partners with private operators for step-down care. HA competes with private hospitals for specialist geriatric beds.

stakeholders.Stakeholder Name: Hong Kong Council of Social Service
stakeholders.Canonical Name: Hong Kong Council of Social Service
stakeholders.Role: Umbrella body coordinating 180+ member agencies, advocates for increased SWD funding and trains staff for elderly centers.

Source metadata.extraction_context: Hong Kong Council of Social Service (HKCSS): Umbrella body coordinating 180+ member agencies. HKCSS advocates for increased SWD funding and trains staff for elderly centers.

stakeholders.Role: Operates 25 residential homes (1,200 beds), receives SWD subsidies but supplements with private donations.

Source metadata.extraction_context: Caritas Hong Kong: Operates 25 residential homes (1,200 beds). Receives SWD subsidies but supplements with private donations. Partners with SWD on community outreach programs.

stakeholders.Role: Runs day care centers for 500 seniors daily; competes with other NGOs for SWD tenders.

Source metadata.extraction_context: Baptist Oi Kwan Social Service: Runs day care centers for 500 seniors daily. Competes with other NGOs for SWD tenders; reports staffing shortages as major pain point.

stakeholders.Role: 300+ facilities (15,000 beds), fully self-funded; regulated by SWD but criticize over-regulation.

Source metadata.extraction_context: Private Residential Care Homes (PRCHs): 300+ facilities (15,000 beds), fully self-funded. Regulated by SWD but criticize over-regulation. Compete directly with subvented NGO homes on pricing.
